summary: Research + verify a guaranteed method to produce an installable iOS .ipa for a Capacitor web-wrapper app WITHOUT owning a Mac; output a concrete step-by-step + a ready GitHub Actions workflow

result.method:
# GUARANTEED PATH: Firas AI on iPhone, No Mac

## 1) Ranked recommendation

### METHOD A (BEST) — GitHub Actions (public repo) builds unsigned .ipa → Sideloadly re-signs on your Windows PC with a free Apple ID

| Hard requirement | Needed? |
|---|---|
| Mac (physical) | NO — a GitHub-hosted **macOS runner** does the `xcodebuild`. You never touch a Mac. |
| Apple **Developer membership** ($99/yr) | NO |
| Apple **ID** (free) | YES — a free Apple ID is required at the *install* step (Sideloadly logs into Apple's portal to mint a 7-day dev cert). |
| Any paid cost | NO — **$0** if the build repo is **public** (macOS CI minutes are free & unlimited on public repos). |
| User action | YES — transfer the .ipa to the PC, install Apple-website iTunes+iCloud, run Sideloadly, enter Apple ID, trust profile, enable Developer Mode. |

Why A wins: it is the only 100%-free, no-Mac path that ends in an app that actually launches on the iPhone. The two halves are cleanly separated — **build** (needs macOS, no Apple account) and **sign+install** (needs free Apple ID, no Mac) — and each half is individually free.

### METHOD B (FALLBACK) — Codemagic free tier builds the .ipa instead of GitHub Actions; same Sideloadly install

| Hard requirement | Needed? |
|---|---|
| Mac | NO — Codemagic runs a cloud Mac mini M2 (500 free min/month). |
| Apple Developer membership | NO (for an unsigned artifact) |
| Apple ID (free) | YES — same install-side requirement as A. |
| Paid cost | NO for the free tier (personal account only, not Team). |
| User action | Same install steps as A. |

Use B only if the GitHub Actions build misbehaves (e.g., private-repo minute limits, or you want Codemagic's documented Capacitor workflow with `xcode-project build-ipa`). The install half is identical. AltStore/AltServer is a further fallback for the install tool if Sideloadly's login fails — it auto-refreshes better but is fussier to set up.

**Do NOT use:** Expo EAS (does not build Capacitor), Ionic Appflow (discontinued), or any "PhoneGap Build" guide (dead since 2020).

**Prerequisite for both (one-time, done on Windows before any of this):** the repo must already be a real Capacitor iOS project — `npx cap add ios` committed, so `ios/App/App.xcworkspace` exists and `npm run build` produces the web assets in your Capacitor `webDir`. The task assumes this is done.

---

## 2) Ready-to-commit workflow — `.github/workflows/ios.yml`

```yaml
name: Build unsigned iOS IPA

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  build-ios-unsigned:
    runs-on: macos-15          # pin explicitly; 'macos-latest' == macos-15 since Sept 2025 (Xcode 16.x)
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm

      - name: Install JS deps
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build web assets
        run: npm run build        # produces the Capacitor webDir; cap sync only COPIES what already exists

      - name: Capacitor sync iOS (copies web assets + runs pod install)
        run: npx cap sync ios

      - name: Archive (unsigned)
        working-directory: ios/App
        run: |
          xcodebuild \
            -workspace App.xcworkspace \
            -scheme App \
            -configuration Release \
            -destination 'generic/platform=iOS' \
            -archivePath "$RUNNER_TEMP/App.xcarchive" \
            clean archive \
            CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO \
            CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO \
            CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY="" \
            CODE_SIGN_ENTITLEMENTS="" \
            AD_HOC_CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=YES

      - name: Package .app into unsigned .ipa
        run: |
          ARCHIVE="$RUNNER_TEMP/App.xcarchive"
          APP=$(ls -d "$ARCHIVE/Products/Applications/"*.app | head -n1)
          echo "Found app: $APP"
          mkdir -p "$RUNNER_TEMP/Payload"
          cp -R "$APP" "$RUNNER_TEMP/Payload/"
          ( cd "$RUNNER_TEMP" && zip -qr App-unsigned.ipa Payload )
          ls -lh "$RUNNER_TEMP/App-unsigned.ipa"

      - name: Upload IPA artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: App-unsigned-ipa
          path: ${{ runner.temp }}/App-unsigned.ipa
          if-no-files-found: error
```

**Load-bearing details (don't "fix" these):**
- All four signing-off flags matter; `-exportArchive` will **NOT** work unsigned — it insists on a signing identity. The manual `Payload/*.app → zip → .ipa` route is exactly why the unsigned path succeeds.
- `.ipa` must be a zip whose **root** holds a `Payload/` folder containing `App.app` — hence `zip` run from `$RUNNER_TEMP`.
- Must be `actions/upload-artifact@v4` (v3 was shut down Jan 30 2025 and hard-fails).
- **Keep the build repo PUBLIC** to guarantee $0. Private-repo Free plan gives only ~200 effective macOS min/month (10× multiplier) ≈ 2 builds — fine for one-off, painful for iteration.

---

## 3) Step-by-step for when you wake up (Windows → iPhone)

**One-time PC setup (the #1 failure point — do this exactly):**
1. **Uninstall** any Microsoft **Store** versions of iTunes and iCloud.
2. Install **iTunes** and **iCloud** from **apple.com** (NOT the Store). The tools need Apple Mobile Device Service + Bonjour drivers that only the apple.com versions provide.
3. Download **Sideloadly** from **sideloadly.io** and install it.

**Get the .ipa off CI:**
4. Open your repo on github.com → **Actions** tab → the latest "Build unsigned iOS IPA" run → scroll to **Artifacts** → download **App-unsigned-ipa** (a zip) → unzip it to get `App-unsigned.ipa`.

**Install onto the iPhone:**
5. Connect iPhone via USB, unlock it, tap **Trust This Computer**.
6. Open Sideloadly. **Drag `App-unsigned.ipa`** into the window.
7. Enter your **free Apple ID** in the Apple Account field → click **Start**.
8. Enter your Apple ID **password** when prompted (goes only to Apple), then the **6-digit 2FA code**. Note: on a free Apple ID, app-specific passwords do NOT work — you must use the live 2FA code flow.
9. When it finishes: on the iPhone go to **Settings → General → VPN & Device Management** → tap your Apple ID profile → **Trust**.
10. **Settings → Privacy & Security → Developer Mode → On → restart** (required on iOS 16+, or the app won't launch).
11. Tap the Firas AI icon. Done.

**The 7-day caveat (unavoidable on a free Apple ID):**
- The signature Sideloadly mints is valid **7 days**. On/before day 7 the app stops launching. To renew: reconnect the iPhone, re-run Sideloadly on the same .ipa (no rebuild needed), re-enter Apple ID. Sideloadly's auto-refresh **daemon** can do this unattended but only while the PC is on, Sideloadly is running, and the phone is reachable over USB/Wi-Fi — treat it as best-effort, plan to re-sign manually ~weekly.
- Other free-tier caps: **max 3** sideloaded apps at once, **max 10** unique App IDs per rolling 7 days.
- Paying Apple $99/yr later would change 7 days → **1 year** and lift the 3-app cap — optional, not required.

**One risk specific to Firas AI:** a free Apple ID cannot provision fancy entitlements (push notifications, app groups, associated domains, iCloud, HealthKit). A plain Capacitor WKWebView shell (which Firas AI is — a web chat app in a webview) signs fine. If you later add a Capacitor push-notification plugin, re-signing on the free ID will fail until you strip that entitlement or go paid.

---

## 4) Honest bottom line

**Yes.** A fully-installed, launchable Firas AI app on your iPhone is achievable with **zero dollars and zero Mac** — provided you accept a **7-day re-sign chore** and use a **free Apple ID** (an Apple *account* is unavoidable; a *paid* one is not; a *Mac* is not).

**The single best path:** commit `.github/workflows/ios.yml` above to a **public** GitHub repo → let the macOS runner spit out `App-unsigned.ipa` for free → download it to your Windows PC → install with **Sideloadly + your free Apple ID**. Re-run Sideloadly weekly to beat the 7-day expiry.

The only thing $0-and-no-Mac cannot buy you is *permanence and effortlessness*: without the $99/yr Apple Developer membership the app expires every 7 days, and without owning a Mac the build must go through cloud CI. Neither blocks you from having the app running on your phone today.

# Sideloadly & AltStore/AltServer on Windows — Research Findings

Both tools do the **same fundamental job**: take an **already-built `.ipa` file** and re-sign it with your Apple ID so iOS will install/run it. Neither one is a build system.
